channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: How did yoga first come to Belgium, and how has it evolved since?
item.description: Originating in India, the practice first became popular among the Belgian upper class.

item.title: Brussels cartoonist pulls plug on Cat Museum project after 12 years
item.description: A dream 12 years in the making hits the brakes: mounting costs force cartoonist Philippe Geluck to withdraw from Brussels' cat museum plans. Officials vow to complete the project regardless.

item.title: Ixelles reduces working week for older people in physically demanding jobs
item.description: "A municipality cannot function without the people who maintain our buildings, prepare meals in schools or carry out many indispensable daily tasks," said the mayor of Ixelles.

item.title: Brussels housing shortage: How Airbnb eats into the city's housing stock
item.description: While short-term rentals are not the main cause for the shortage of affordable housing in Belgium, they "contribute to the strain on the housing market."

item.title: More Belgians are living alone as household sizes continue to shrink
item.description: The trend is particularly visible in Brussels, where nearly one household in two is occupied by a single resident.

item.title: What to do in Brussels this weekend: 12 - 14 June
item.description: Summer is in full swing. From blooming roses to open-air festivals, here's what's happening in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: Mostly cloudy on Friday with occasional light rain
item.description: Light coat rain might be needed if you're walking around Brussels.

item.title: The population of Brussels grew by only 39 people last year – here's why
item.description: According to newly released figures by Statbel, Belgium's statistics agency, the population of Brussels is stagnating.

item.title: Brussels to ban shared e-scooters from 2027
item.description: Beyond safety concerns, the government also pointed to the growing nuisance caused by improperly parked scooters.

item.title: Meise Botanical Garden opens new area with 175 endangered species
item.description: The new Native Flora Garden is a living catalogue of Belgian flora, where visitors can stroll through coastal dunes and salt marshes to heathland, fens, grasslands and forests.

item.title: Brussels takes legal action against disruptive 'aeroplane motorway'
item.description: Some of the country's most densely populated municipalities have complained for several months about the noise of landing aircraft every four minutes.

item.title: French far-right MEP Jordan Bardella to speak in Brussels on Thursday
item.description: Vlaams Belang and the National Rally go back a long way. "Both parties are among the oldest radical right-wing movements in Europe."

item.title: Nearly 2.3 million passengers passed through Brussels Airport in May
item.description: Brussels Airport saw a 3.2% increase in passenger numbers despite a nationwide protest on 12 May and disruptions in the Middle East.

item.title: Fire breaks out at Place Jourdan brasserie in Brussels
item.description: Three staff members were treated at the scene by the emergency services, but did not require hospitalisation.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 13 to 14 June
item.description: From film nights to indoor-outdoor comedy theatre, here are all the best activities to do with your family in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: 'Part of our DNA': Brussels protects city's nightlife amid series of nightclub closures
item.description: "The principle is simple: whoever causes the change takes responsibility. If you move in next to an existing club, you must take measures against noise nuisance."

item.title: Road closures and tram disruptions: What to expect during this summer's construction work
item.description: Brussels residents have been warned to expect disruptions this summer as the city ramps up its construction projects.

item.title: Brussels' Palais de Justice slowly breaking free of scaffolding
item.description: "An entire generation of Belgians has never really known the Palais de Justice without scaffolding. People aged 40 or over have seen it hidden behind a metal cage for almost their entire lives."

item.title: USG must pay €3,500 fine for pyrotechnics during Belgian cup final
item.description: In the 18th, 68th and 96th minutes, USG fans set off their fireworks, totalling at least 27 items.

item.title: Over €8 million seized in major money-laundering investigation in Brussels
item.description: The money is believed to have originated from drug trafficking in France, before being brought to Belgium by money couriers.
